criteria_results.description: useManualSave.ts contains retry logic that catches AbortError and retries the save request up to 3 times with exponential backoff
criteria_results.evidence: useManualSave.ts implements performSave with a retryCount parameter, catching AbortError (e.name === 'AbortError' || e.message?.includes('aborted')) and retrying up to 3 times with exponential backoff (Math.pow(2, retryCount) * 1000).

criteria_results.description: Save hook does not abort pending requests when Page Visibility changes to hidden on mobile
criteria_results.evidence: useManualSave.ts and useAutoSave.ts have visibilitychange listeners that log or trigger saves instead of aborting. apiRequest in queryClient.ts only aborts on its own 30s timeout.

criteria_results.description: User-friendly error toast is shown instead of technical 'signal is aborted' message
criteria_results.evidence: useManualSave.ts replaces the technical error message with a user-friendly one and shows a 'Connection weak' toast during retry attempts.

criteria_results.description: MobileBottomBar save action properly awaits the save completion before showing success state
criteria_results.evidence: MobileBottomBar.tsx (line 202) uses 'await handleSave(true)' inside an onClick handler that is NOT marked as 'async', causing a TypeScript error: "'await' expressions are only allowed within async functions". This prevents the code from running and doesn't properly await.

criteria_results.description: No AbortController.abort() is called during active save operations when component unmounts on mobile
criteria_results.evidence: useManualSave.ts and useAutoSave.ts useEffect cleanup functions do not call abort() on any active requests; they only clean up timeouts and event listeners.

blocking_issues: Syntax error in client/src/components/editor/MobileBottomBar.tsx: The onClick handler for status update buttons (around line 191) is missing the 'async' keyword, making the 'await handleSave(true)' call invalid.
Inconsistent implementation: ObjectPhotoGallery.tsx and PMObjectDetailsPanel.tsx also call handleSave(true) without 'await', which may lead to unhandled promise rejections if the save fails.
suggestions: Add a loading indicator to the status buttons in MobileBottomBar and PMObjectDetailsPanel to provide visual feedback while the save/retry is in progress.
Ensure that all components calling handleSave properly await the promise to maintain consistent state tracking.
next_focus: Fix the syntax error in MobileBottomBar.tsx by adding the 'async' keyword to the status button onClick handlers and ensure all handleSave(true) calls are properly awaited across the mobile components.
